description: Publish request whose body.agent_id is not did:web. RFC-ACDP-0001 §5.4 mandates did:web for v0.1.0 agent_ids; the schema's `did` definition is intentionally loose to permit non-did:web values in contributors/audience, so registries MUST enforce the method on agent_id at the validation layer (RFC-ACDP-0003 §2.1 step 1 schema validation, or step 6 key resolution). The preferred error is schema_violation; key_not_authorized is also conformant if the registry discovers the mismatch later in the pipeline.

expected.rationale: schema_violation is the preferred response — registries should reject before attempting DID resolution. key_not_authorized is acceptable for registries that discover the mismatch in step 6 (DID resolution). Either response code is conformant; no other code is.

notes: v0.1.0 producers MUST use did:web (RFC-ACDP-0001 §5.4 DID method scope table). Registries advertising additional DID methods in supported_did_methods MAY accept those methods on agent_id only after a future protocol version explicitly extends the scope.
